proverb_of_day.note: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge.
Seek her as silver, search for her as hidden treasure.
Trust in the LORD with all your heart.
Keep your heart with all vigilance.
Rejoice in the wife of your youth.
Go to the ant, O sluggard; consider her ways and be wise.
Keep my commandments and live.
Wisdom calls aloud in the street.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom.
A wise son makes a glad father.
Pride brings disgrace; with humility comes wisdom.
Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge.
He who walks with the wise becomes wise.
The wise of heart will receive commandments.
A soft answer turns away wrath.
Commit your work to the LORD, and your plans will be established.
A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
The name of the LORD is a strong tower.
Better the poor whose walk is blameless than a fool whose lips are perverse.
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler.
The king's heart is a stream of water in the hand of the LORD.
A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches.
Do not toil to acquire wealth.
By wisdom a house is built, and by understanding it is established.
Like apples of gold in settings of silver is a word fitly spoken.
Answer not a fool according to his folly.
As iron sharpens iron, so one man sharpens another.
The wicked flee when none pursue; the righteous are bold as a lion.
He who is often reproved, yet stiffens his neck, will suddenly be broken.
Every word of God proves true.
An excellent wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels.
